title: value contextual keyword should be preferred
description: "value" contextual keyword should be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen you need to get external input forsetandinitmethods defined for properties and indexers or forremoveandaddmethods for events, you should always get this input throught thevaluecontextual keyword.The contextual keywordvalueis similar to an input parameter of a method; it references the value that the client code is attempting to assign to the property, indexer or event.The keywordvalueholds the value the accessor was called with. Not using it means that the accessor ignores the caller’s intent which could cause unexpected results at runtime.Noncompliant code exampleprivate int count; public int Count { get { return count; } set { count = 42; } //
recommendation: Remove the problematic code and refactor as needed.

logic.detection_conditions: Detects property setters or event accessors that don't use the 'value' keyword

checks.message: Property setter does not use 'value' keyword - ignores assigned value
Expression-bodied setter does not use 'value' keyword
Event 'add' accessor does not use 'value' keyword
Event 'remove' accessor does not use 'value' keyword

logic.triggering_conditions: set { } without 'value' OR add { } without 'value' OR remove { } without 'value'
logic.exclusion_conditions: Setters/accessors that do use 'value' (via negative lookahead)
logic.reasoning: The 'value' contextual keyword represents the value being assigned. Not using it means the setter ignores what the caller is trying to assign, which is almost always a bug or design mistake.
